title: Replace a nested case with a three-clause with
prompt:
The function below has nested `case` calls that return `{:error, reason}` shapes. Rewrite as a `with` expression that short-circuits to the first error.

```
def transfer(from_id, to_id, amount) do
  case MyApp.Accounts.fetch(from_id) do
    {:ok, from} ->
      case MyApp.Accounts.fetch(to_id) do
        {:ok, to} ->
          case MyApp.Ledger.move(from, to, amount) do
            {:ok, txn} -> {:ok, txn}
            err -> err
          end
        err -> err
      end
    err -> err
  end
end
```

Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Bank do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Three-clause nest. Tempting to keep an `else` clause but the pass-through nature means `with` without `else` is cleanest.
